evals.prompt: How do I set up Lead Catcher in Mailshake?
evals.expected_output: Guide to enabling and configuring Lead Catcher with assignment rules and lead status workflow

assertions.name: setup_steps
assertions.description: Provides steps to enable Lead Catcher and configure auto-assignment rules

assertions.name: lead_statuses
assertions.description: Explains lead statuses: open, won/closed, ignored, lost

assertions.name: crm_distinction
assertions.description: Clarifies that Lead Catcher is not a CRM replacement — leads should be pushed to CRM for pipeline management

evals.prompt: I need to add 500 recipients via CSV to Mailshake. What format does the CSV need to be in?
evals.expected_output: CSV format requirements and import guidance including required fields, merge field mapping, and plan considerations

assertions.name: required_fields
assertions.description: States that emailAddress (email) is the only required field

assertions.name: csv_structure
assertions.description: Explains CSV structure — headers in first row, recommended columns (first, last, company, title), custom columns become merge fields

assertions.name: verification_warning
assertions.description: Warns that Mailshake does not verify emails on upload and recommends pre-verification

evals.prompt: I want to use the Mailshake API to create a campaign and add recipients programmatically. We expect about 200 new leads per day.
evals.expected_output: API guidance for campaign creation and recipient management with rate limit awareness

assertions.name: api_reference
assertions.description: Points to references/mailshake-api-reference.md for full API docs

assertions.name: rate_limits
assertions.description: Warns about 100 requests/minute rate limit and recommends batching for 200 leads/day
